title: Infinite login redirect loop after Okta-to-Entra ID SSO migration due to SameSite cookie rejection and email claim mismatch
problem: After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users are stuck in an infinite redirect loop: they authenticate successfully with Entra, get redirected back to TaskFlow with a valid token and Set-Cookie header, but the session cookie is not saved by the browser, causing TaskFlow to treat them as unauthenticated and redirect them back to Microsoft.
root_cause_hypothesis: Two compounding issues: (1) TaskFlow's session cookie likely has SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict, which causes browsers to reject it when set during a cross-site redirect from login.microsoftonline.com. With Okta, the flow may have worked differently (e.g., different redirect mechanics or the cookie attribute wasn't an issue). This is the primary cause of the redirect loop. (2) For a subset of affected users, even if the cookie issue is resolved, an email claim mismatch will prevent session creation — Entra sends the primary email (e.g., jane@company.com) but TaskFlow has the user stored under a plus-sign variant (jane+taskflow@company.com) or a pre-migration domain (bob@oldcompany.com), causing the account lookup to fail.
reproduction_steps: Have a user whose TaskFlow account was created with a plus-sign email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or a pre-migration email domain attempt to log in
User clicks login on taskflow.company.com and is redirected to login.microsoftonline.com
User authenticates successfully with Entra ID
User is redirected back to taskflow.company.com with a valid token
Observe in browser dev tools: Set-Cookie header is present in the response but the cookie is not persisted by the browser
TaskFlow treats the user as unauthenticated and redirects back to Microsoft, creating an infinite loop
Optionally verify: clearing cookies may temporarily fix it, incognito may work inconsistently
environment: TaskFlow hosted at taskflow.company.com over HTTPS. SSO recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID (login.microsoftonline.com). Issue occurs across Chrome and Edge. Affects ~30% of users, including those with plus-sign email addresses and those whose email domains changed during migration.
impact: Approximately 30% of the team is completely unable to log into TaskFlow through normal means. There is no reliable workaround — incognito and cookie clearing are inconsistent. This blocks affected users from accessing the application entirely.
recommended_fix: Address both root causes: (1) **Cookie fix (primary):** Inspect TaskFlow's session cookie attributes. Set `SameSite=None; Secure` on the session cookie so it is accepted during the cross-site redirect back from login.microsoftonline.com. Alternatively, implement a POST-based redirect landing page or an intermediate same-origin callback that sets the cookie outside of the cross-site redirect context. (2) **Email matching fix (secondary):** Update the user lookup logic during SSO authentication to normalize email addresses — strip plus-sign suffixes before matching, and/or match against known email aliases. Consider adding a migration script to reconcile stored emails with Entra ID primary emails for users whose domains changed (e.g., bob@oldcompany.com → bob@company.com).
proposed_test_case: Create a test user in TaskFlow with a plus-sign email address (test+alias@company.com). Configure Entra ID to return the primary email without the plus-sign part. Initiate the SSO login flow and verify: (a) the session cookie is persisted by the browser after the cross-site redirect, (b) the user is matched to their TaskFlow account despite the email format difference, (c) the user lands on the authenticated dashboard without any redirect loop, and (d) subsequent login attempts in the same browser session also succeed without looping.
information_gaps: Exact SameSite attribute value currently set on TaskFlow's session cookie — needs server-side inspection
Whether TaskFlow's auth code handles the token-to-session creation in the redirect response itself or via a subsequent same-origin request
Complete list of affected users cross-referenced with their stored email vs. Entra ID primary email to confirm the mismatch theory for non-plus-sign users
TaskFlow server-side authentication logs showing the exact point of failure (cookie rejection vs. account lookup failure) per user
